canonical_definition: Based on the provided data, Professionalism is defined as a latent attribute of pre-training text, indicating the extent to which it contains professional knowledge. One paper specifies this knowledge as the type found in "textbooks or research articles" and frames it as a dimension that "serves as an indicator of the level of professional knowledge contained in the text" ("PrivacyRestore: Privacy-Preserving Inference in Large Language Models via Privacy Removal and Restoration"). This work also suggests that Professionalism supports advanced model capabilities in knowledge-intensive tasks. A separate paper proposes a causal relationship where human preference alignment is reported to cause Professionalism. The provided materials do not contain information on how this construct is operationalized or measured.
papers: Enhancing Open-Domain Task-Solving Capability ofLLMs via Autonomous Tool Integration fromGitHub
PrivacyRestore: Privacy-Preserving Inference in Large Language Models via Privacy Removal and Restoration
SAMDecoding: Speculative Decoding via Suffix Automaton

definitions.text: The latent extent to which pre-training text contains professional knowledge, such as that found in textbooks or research articles, which supports advanced model capabilities in knowledge-intensive tasks.
definitions.paper: PrivacyRestore: Privacy-Preserving Inference in Large Language Models via Privacy Removal and Restoration

relationships.paper: Towards Federated RLHF with Aggregated Client Preference for LLMs
